title: Add digital signature functionality for network configurations and security policies

requirements.summary: Add a 'Sign Policy' button to each PolicyCard component that calls the backend sign function and updates the UI to show signature status
acceptanceCriteria: Each policy card displays a 'Sign Policy' button
Button is disabled if policy is already signed
Clicking the button triggers the sign mutation
UI shows loading state during signing
Signed status is visually indicated with an icon or badge

file_operations.description: Add a 'Sign Policy' button that calls the useSignPolicy mutation hook. Display button with loading state during signing. Disable button if policy.digitalSignature is present. Show signed status with a checkmark icon or badge when policy has a digitalSignature. Import and use the useSignPolicy hook and display toast notifications on success/error.

requirements.summary: Display signature verification status on the SecurityPolicies page showing whether each policy's signature is valid, invalid, or unsigned
acceptanceCriteria: Each policy card shows signature status with color-coded badge
Status is automatically verified when policies are loaded
Status updates when policies are modified or signed

file_operations.description: Add verification status display to PolicyCard. Call useVerifyPolicySignature query hook for each policy to get validation status. Display color-coded badge: 'Signed & Valid' (green) when signature is valid, 'Invalid Signature' (red) when signature verification fails, 'Unsigned' (gray) when no digitalSignature exists. Handle loading and error states for verification queries.

Ensure the page properly passes policy data including digitalSignature field to PolicyCard components. The verification status will automatically update when policies are refetched after signing operations due to cache invalidation.

requirements.summary: Create React Query hooks for signing policies and verifying policy signatures with proper cache invalidation
acceptanceCriteria: useSignPolicy mutation hook calls the backend sign function
useVerifyPolicySignature query hook calls the backend verify function
Signing a policy invalidates the policies query cache
Hooks follow the same pattern as existing useQueries hooks

file_operations.description: Add useSignPolicy mutation hook that calls actor.storePolicySignature with policyId and generated signature. Invalidate 'policies' query key on success. Add useVerifyPolicySignature query hook that calls actor.getPolicySignature and compares it with a locally computed signature to determine validity. Follow existing patterns from useAddThreat and useGetAllPolicies hooks. Both hooks should accept the actor from useActor hook and use queryClient for cache invalidation.
